title: Quantum Benchmarking Initiative expands quest to separate hype from reality | DARPA
why:
- The Quantum Benchmarking Initiative (QBI) is expanding its efforts to rigorously determine whether any quantum computing architecture can achieve utility-scale operation (where computational value exceeds cost) by 2033.
- A new solicitation invites organizations not previously funded by QBI, especially those with distinct and untested approaches, to participate in the evaluation process.
- Since mid-2024, QBI has evaluated approaches from 20 commercial companies using a variety of qubit architectures.
- Eleven organizations have advanced to Stage B for deeper technical risk-reduction and development planning; two have advanced to system-level validation in Stage C.
- The initiative underscores rapid progress in the field and the likelihood that a utility-scale quantum computer may be built by 2033, but emphasizes the continued uncertainty about which approach or team will succeed.
